raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, a fraudulent transaction in the amount of {$1100.00} from retailer XXXX XXXX was charged to a card connected to my Amex account that I never had possession or knowledge of. ( Case # XXXX ) I filed a dispute with Amex detailing how I did not authorize, purchase, or receive any products from XXXX XXXX and was unaware that I had an additional card until the fraud activity occurred.

As the case was closed by Amex I was notified that they would be re-debiting the transaction citing that the reason was because I provided the merchant with my financial information.

I attempted to email, chat ( per Amex direction ), and called twice in an effort to resolve this. In my chat session, the agent noted that something was strange about my dispute as it was showing that it was initially debited twice but only credited once. ( I'm not clear on what that means ).

In a phone conversation with an Amex agent on XX/XX/year> I explained that there is another address that gets confused with mine as it has the same address number and nearly identical street name ( mine is XXXX  XXXX XXXX , the other is XXXX  XXXX XXXX ) and that delivery often get mistakenly sent there. I asked to review the picture of the residence the package was delivered to and the agent was unable to provide me with it. I asked if I could send her a picture of my front door to see if it matched and she told me I could not. Her suggestion was to contact law enforcement to claim a theft occurred but I explained that I have no information or delivery information to file a case.

On the credit card statement I attached there are two names. " XXXX XXXX XXXX '' is my Amex card I use regularly. " XXXX XXXX '' is used to reference the additional card I did not have in my possession and I was unaware existed. On the statement you will see on page 4 the transaction for {$1100.00} under " XXXX XXXX '' from XXXX XXXX. On page 3 you will see the credit from Amex for this transaction.

Ultimately, despite my efforts, I am being made responsible for a charge to a card I didn't know existed for a package I never received.
